modules.description: Owns authentication, current-user resolution, login/register flows, and auth policy helpers.
notes: AuthController should remain thin and depend on public auth services only.
Current-user resolution should not depend on Analysis pipeline internals.

modules.description: Owns the analysis facade, analysis pipeline, ordered steps, UAO processing, and AnalysisResult production.
notes: IAnalysisPipeline is an application boundary and should not leak pipeline step internals.
Steps must remain internal and AnalysisExecutionContext is the only shared step state object.

modules.description: Owns course retrieval, course administration helpers, and course material orchestration entry points.
notes: If dedicated interfaces are added later, controller dependencies should switch to those interfaces.

modules.description: Owns OCR service contracts, OCR request models, and provider adapters.
forbiddenDependencies: Persistence direct access except through accepted service boundaries
notes: Controllers and analysis code should use IOCRService, not concrete OCR providers.

modules.description: Owns LLM public service contracts, gateway integration, and request execution plumbing.
forbiddenDependencies: ApplicationDbContext outside accepted gateway logging boundary
Direct domain persistence outside module contracts
notes: Analysis code should depend on ILLMService or ILLMModule, not LLMGateway directly.

modules.description: Owns knowledge retrieval, normalization helpers, and learning path support.
notes: Knowledge normalization should remain reusable without depending on pipeline step implementations.

modules.description: Owns question management and question lookup boundaries.
notes: QuestionService currently lives under Services/Knowledge but is treated as its own conceptual module boundary.

modules.description: Owns visualization projection helpers and visualization persistence boundary adapters.
notes: Visualization services may persist through IPersistenceService but should not import EF Core directly.

modules.description: Owns EF Core, ApplicationDbContext access, persistence queries/commands, entity loading, and migrations.
notes: This is the only module allowed to touch EF Core and ApplicationDbContext directly.

modules.description: Owns semantic cross-module data structures shared without infrastructure coupling.
notes: SharedKernel should stay free of ASP.NET, EF Core, and provider implementation details.
